primary_reason: In 50% of observed sessions where 'Exploitation' was the last detected stage, 'Privilege Escalation' was the next attacker action — this is the strongest transition signal driving the prediction.
transition_story: After observing 'Reconnaissance → Credential Access → Exploitation', the model noted that in 50% of historical attack sessions where an attacker was actively exploiting vulnerable services, the next observed action was attempting to gain elevated/admin privileges. The second most likely step was 'Persistence' (28%).

matched_pattern.name: Classic Credential Pivot
matched_pattern.description: Most common enterprise intrusion pattern — recon leads to credential theft, then exploitation of the gained access.
matched_pattern.prevalence: Seen in 68% of enterprise breaches (Verizon DBIR)

counterfactual: If 'Reconnaissance' had been blocked, the predicted 'Privilege Escalation' would not have been reached, reducing threat score by approximately 45 points.

primary_reason: In 28% of observed sessions where 'Lateral Movement' was the last detected stage, 'Command & Control' was the next attacker action — this is the strongest transition signal driving the prediction.
transition_story: After observing 'Persistence → Privilege Escalation → Lateral Movement', the model noted that in 28% of historical attack sessions where an attacker was moving across the network to new hosts, the next observed action was establishing a covert communication channel. The second most likely step was 'Command & Control' (28%).

matched_pattern.name: APT Lateral Spread
matched_pattern.description: Classic APT progression after initial foothold — escalate locally, then pivot across the network.
matched_pattern.prevalence: Characteristic of APT28, APT29, Lazarus Group

counterfactual: If 'Reconnaissance' had been blocked, the predicted 'Command & Control' would not have been reached, reducing threat score by approximately 55 points.

primary_reason: In 70% of observed sessions where 'Credential Access' was the last detected stage, 'Data Exfiltration' was the next attacker action — this is the strongest transition signal driving the prediction.

The second most likely step was 'Privilege Escalation' (32%).
